message:
docs(config): add comprehensive rustdoc for all major config structs

Add defaults tables and usage examples to ServerConfig, AppConfig,
CorsConfig, RateLimitConfig, JsonConfig, and MultipartConfig. Close
Phase 6 umbrella (bd-11ka) with all children complete.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-12f8","title":"OpenAPI: JsonSchema impl for numeric types","description":"Missing JsonSchema implementations for i8, i16, i32, i64, u8, u16, u32, u64, f32, f64, isize, usize. Core numeric types must have schema support.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:52:22.979175597Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:18:38.429877290Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:18:32.905090162Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-12f8","depends_on_id":"bd-1vpz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:56:18.234247236Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":10,"issue_id":"bd-12f8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Background & Context
OpenAPI schemas require proper type definitions for all Rust primitives. Currently missing JsonSchema implementations for numeric types, which are among the most commonly used in APIs.

## Missing Implementations
Need JsonSchema for:
- Signed integers: i8, i16, i32, i64, i128, isize
- Unsigned integers: u8, u16, u32, u64, u128, usize
- Floating point: f32, f64
- Non-zero types: NonZeroI32, NonZeroU64, etc.

## OpenAPI Schema Mapping
```json
// i32, i64, isize -> 
{ "type": "integer", "format": "int32" }  // or int64

// u32, u64, usize ->
{ "type": "integer", "format": "int32", "minimum": 0 }

// f32 ->
{ "type": "number", "format": "float" }

// f64 ->
{ "type": "number", "format": "double" }
```

## Implementation Pattern
```rust
impl JsonSchema for i32 {
    fn schema_name() -> String {
        "int32".to_string()
    }
    
    fn json_schema(_gen: &mut SchemaGenerator) -> Schema {
        Schema::Object(SchemaObject {
            instance_type: Some(SingleOrVec::Single(Box::new(InstanceType::Integer))),
            format: Some("int32".to_string()),
            ..Default::default()
        })
    }
}
```

## Files to Modify
- crates/fastapi-openapi/src/lib.rs - Add impls
- crates/fastapi-openapi/src/impls.rs (may create for organization)

## Dependencies
- Needed by bd-20ff (enum schema) for enum discriminants
- Needed by bd-3i50 (constraints) for min/max values

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All primitive numeric types have JsonSchema impl
- [ ] Correct OpenAPI format strings (int32, int64, float, double)
- [ ] Unsigned types have minimum: 0
- [ ] Tests verify schema output for each type
","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:54:23Z"},{"id":21,"issue_id":"bd-12f8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented JsonSchema for all numeric types: i8, i16, i32, i64, i128, isize, u8, u16, u32, u64, u128, usize, f32, f64. Added NonZero variants. Unsigned types have minimum:0, NonZero unsigned have minimum:1. Added 7 comprehensive tests covering all types and serialization. All 35 tests pass, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-01-28T03:18:38Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-14bc","title":"Test BackgroundTasks error isolation","description":"## Task
Verify that background task panics/errors don't crash the server or affect other requests.

## Implementation
- Queue task that panics
- Queue task that returns error
- Verify server continues operating
- Verify error is logged

## Test Cases
1. Task panic caught and logged
2. Task error caught and logged
3. Other queued tasks still execute
4. Server handles next request normally
5. No resource leaks from failed tasks

## Location

 {"id":"bd-nww8","title":"Rich Output Integration: Premium Console UI with Agent Compatibility","description":"# Rich Output Integration Epic

## Overview

This epic encompasses the complete integration of rich_rust (a Rust port of Python's Rich library) 
throughout the fastapi_rust codebase to provide premium, stylish console output for human observers 
while maintaining full compatibility with AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Aider, etc.).

## The Problem We're Solving

Currently, fastapi_rust has minimal console output - basic println! statements and simple text.
This creates several issues:

1. **Poor Developer Experience**: Humans watching the framework in action see plain, hard-to-parse text
2. **Missed Branding Opportunity**: No visual identity or professional polish
3. **Debugging Difficulty**: Errors, routes, and middleware are hard to visually parse
4. **No Structure**: Complex data (route tables, dependency trees) displayed as flat text

## The Solution

Integrate rich_rust to provide:
- Beautiful tables for routes, middleware, dependencies
- Colored HTTP method indicators (GET=blue, POST=green, DELETE=red)
- Styled error messages with hierarchical location display
- Progress indicators for startup and shutdown
- Panels and boxes for grouped information
- Syntax-highlighted JSON body previews

## Critical Design Constraint: Agent Compatibility

**The primary users of fastapi_rust are AI coding agents.** These agents:
- Parse stdout/stderr to understand program output
- Cannot process ANSI escape codes meaningfully
- Need structured, predictable text formats
- May be running in CI/CD environments without TTY

Therefore, the integration MUST implement dual-mode output:
- **Rich Mode**: Full rich_rust styling (for humans watching)
- **Plain Mode**: Structured plain text (for agents)

Detection heuristics for agent mode:
- Environment variables: CLAUDE_CODE, CODEX_CLI, CURSOR_SESSION, AIDER_SESSION, AGENT_MODE, CI
- NO_COLOR standard (disables colors)
- Not a TTY (stdout piped)

## Architecture Overview

New crate: fastapi-output
- Agent detection logic
- Mode switching (Rich/Plain/Minimal)
- RichOutput facade API
- Theme system
- Component renderers

## Theme System

FastAPI-inspired color palette:
- Primary: #009688 (teal)
- Secondary: #4CAF50 (green)  
- Accent: #FF9800 (orange)
- HTTP methods: GET=#61AFFE, POST=#49CC90, PUT=#FCA130, DELETE=#F93E3E, PATCH=#50E3C2
- Status codes: 2xx=green, 3xx=cyan, 4xx=yellow, 5xx=red

## Integration Points (All Major Components)

1. App Startup: Banner, route table, middleware stack, config summary
2. Request Logging: Method colors, path, status, timing
3. Error Display: Validation errors, HTTP errors with context
4. Testing Output: Test results with pass/fail indicators
5. Shutdown: Progress display for graceful shutdown phases
6. HTTP Parser: Debug output with parsed request details
7. Router: Route tree visualization
8. OpenAPI: Spec summary display
9. Dependencies: Dependency injection tree

## Success Criteria

1. Human observers see beautiful, professional console output
2. AI agents receive clean, parseable plain text
3. Mode switching is automatic and reliable
4. Zero performance impact in plain mode
5. Configurable via environment variables
6. Comprehensive test coverage for both modes

## Dependencies

- rich_rust library (from /dp/rich_rust)
- crossterm (for terminal detection)

## Estimated Scope

- 1 new crate (fastapi-output)
- ~15-20 integration points across existing crates
- ~2000-3000 lines of new code
- 6 implementation phases","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:02:31.887842522Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T17:34:54.786098475Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T17:34:54.786033354Z","close_reason":"Rich output integration complete:
- fastapi-output crate with 20+ tests passing
- Agent detection: Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Aider, Windsurf, Cline, Copilot
- CI detection: GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Jenkins, CircleCI, Travis, Buildkite
- Output modes: Rich (humans), Plain (agents), Minimal (simple terminals)
- Environment variable configuration (FASTAPI_OUTPUT_MODE, FASTAPI_AGENT_MODE)
- FastAPI-themed color palette
- Components: route tables, middleware stack, HTTP inspector, error display
- Comprehensive dual-mode testing","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["integration","rich-output"]}
 {"id":"bd-o4r9","title":"Implement HTTP response inspector","description":"## Task: HTTP Response Inspector

### Parent Feature
Phase 4: HTTP & Router Integration (bd-1hu2)

### Overview
Create a visual HTTP response inspector that displays response details in a structured, color-coded format for debugging.

### Background & Reasoning
Debugging response issues requires clear visibility into:
- Status codes (with semantic coloring)
- Response headers
- Body content and format
- Response size and timing

### Technical Implementation

#### Data Structure
```rust
// crates/fastapi-output/src/components/http_inspector.rs

pub struct ResponseInspector<'a> {
    response: &'a Response,
    show_body: bool,
    body_max_len: usize,
    show_timing: bool,
}
```

#### Visualization

**Rich Mode**:
```
┌────────────────── HTTP Response ─────────────────┐
│                                                   │
│  200 OK                                           │
│                                                   │
│  Headers                                          │
│  ┌─────────────────────┬────────────────────────┐│
│  │ Content-Type        │ application/json       ││
│  │ Content-Length      │ 256                    ││
│  │ X-Request-ID        │ abc123                 ││
│  │ X-Response-Time     │ 15ms                   ││
│  └─────────────────────┴────────────────────────┘│
│                                                   │
│  Body (256 bytes, JSON)                          │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐│
│  │ {                                            ││
│  │   "id": 42,                                 ││
│  │   "name": "Alice",                         ││
│  │   "created_at": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"     ││
│  │ }                                            ││
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────┘│
│                                                   │
│  Total time: 15.234ms                            │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

#### Status Code Coloring
```rust
impl FastApiTheme {
    pub fn style_status_code(&self, status: u16) -> String {
        let color = match status {
            100..=199 => "cyan",       // Informational
            200..=299 => "green",      // Success
            300..=399 => "yellow",     // Redirect
            400..=499 => "red",        // Client error
            500..=599 => "red bold",   // Server error
            _ => "white",
        };
        
        let reason = status_reason(status);
        self.style_text(&format!("{} {}", status, reason), color)
    }
}
```

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ResponseInspector struct with builder pattern
- [ ] Status code coloring by category
- [ ] Headers table display
- [ ] Body preview with JSON formatting
- [ ] Response timing display
- [ ] Plain text fallback
- [ ] Feature-gated
- [ ] Unit tests

### Dependencies
- bd-36uw (RichOutput facade)
- bd-14v4 (FastApiTheme)
- bd-1hu2 (Phase 4 feature)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:15:44.356887504Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T02:09:51.598397961Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T02:09:51.598333020Z","close_reason":"Implementation complete - all acceptance criteria met: ResponseInspector with builder pattern, status code coloring by category, headers table display, body preview, response timing, plain text fallback, runtime feature gating via debug mode, and 8 unit tests passing","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-o4r9","depends_on_id":"bd-14v4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-o4r9","depends_on_id":"bd-1hu2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-o4r9","depends_on_id":"bd-36uw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-pto1","title":"Write documentation and examples","description":"## Task: Documentation and Examples (REVISED)

### Overview
Create comprehensive documentation and examples for the rich output system using the ACTUAL implemented API.

### API Reference (rustdoc)

The main entry points are already documented in lib.rs:
- `RichOutput` - Main facade for output operations
- `OutputMode` - Three modes: Rich, Plain, Minimal
- `Banner` - Server startup banner
- `ErrorFormatter` - Validation and HTTP error formatting
- `RequestLogger` - Request/response logging

### Getting Started Guide

```markdown
# Getting Started with fastapi-output

## Installation

Add to your Cargo.toml:
\`\`\`toml
[dependencies]
fastapi-output = { path = "../crates/fastapi-output" }
\`\`\`

## Basic Usage

### Auto-Detecting Mode
\`\`\`rust
use fastapi_output::prelude::*;

fn main() {
    // Automatically detects agent/CI environment
    let output = RichOutput::auto();

    // Check what mode was selected
    println!("Mode: {}", output.mode_name());
    println!("Agent-friendly: {}", output.is_agent_mode());

    // Print styled messages
    output.success("Server started successfully");
    output.info("Listening on port 8000");
    output.warning("Using default configuration");
    output.error("Failed to connect to database");
}
\`\`\`

### Startup Banner
\`\`\`rust
use fastapi_output::prelude::*;

let output = RichOutput::auto();
let banner = Banner::new(output.mode());

let info = ServerInfo::new(env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION"), "localhost", 8000)
    .docs_path("/docs")
    .redoc_path("/redoc")
    .openapi_path("/openapi.json");

println!("{}", banner.render(&info));
\`\`\`

Output (Plain Mode - for agents):
\`\`\`
================================================================================
                              FastAPI Rust v0.1.0
================================================================================
Server URL: http://localhost:8000
Documentation:
  - Swagger UI:  /docs
  - ReDoc:       /redoc
  - OpenAPI:     /openapi.json
================================================================================
\`\`\`

### Request Logging
\`\`\`rust
use fastapi_output::prelude::*;
use std::time::Duration;

let logger = RequestLogger::new(OutputMode::Plain);

let entry = LogEntry::new(HttpMethod::Get, "/api/users", 200)
    .timing(ResponseTiming::new(Duration::from_millis(45)))
    .client_ip("127.0.0.1");

println!("{}", logger.format(&entry));
// Output: GET /api/users 200 45ms 127.0.0.1
\`\`\`

### Error Formatting
\`\`\`rust
use fastapi_output::prelude::*;

let formatter = ErrorFormatter::new(OutputMode::Plain);

// Validation errors
let errors = vec![
    ValidationErrorDetail::new(
        vec![LocItem::field("body"), LocItem::field("email")],
        "invalid email format",
        "value_error.email",
    ),
];
let result = formatter.format_validation_errors(&errors);
println!("{}", result.plain);

// HTTP errors
let http_err = HttpErrorInfo::new(404, "User not found")
    .path("/api/users/999")
    .method("GET");
println!("{}", formatter.format_http_error(&http_err).plain);
\`\`\`
\`\`\`

### Agent Compatibility Guide

#### How Detection Works

The library checks environment variables in this order:

1. `FASTAPI_OUTPUT_MODE` - Explicit mode (rich/plain/minimal)
2. `FASTAPI_AGENT_MODE=1` - Force agent mode
3. `FASTAPI_HUMAN_MODE=1` - Force human mode
4. `FORCE_COLOR` - Force rich output
5. `NO_COLOR` - Force plain output
6. Agent detection (CLAUDE_CODE, CODEX_CLI, CURSOR_SESSION, etc.)
7. CI detection (CI, GITHUB_ACTIONS, GITLAB_CI, etc.)
8. TTY detection

#### Forcing a Mode
\`\`\`rust
// Always use plain mode
let output = RichOutput::plain();

// Always use rich mode
let output = RichOutput::rich();

// Auto-detect based on environment
let output = RichOutput::auto();

// Change mode at runtime
let mut output = RichOutput::auto();
output.set_mode(OutputMode::Plain);
\`\`\`

#### Plain Mode Output Format

Plain mode uses consistent prefixes for easy parsing:
- `[OK]` - Success messages
- `[ERROR]` - Error messages
- `[WARN]` - Warning messages
- `[INFO]` - Informational messages
- `[DEBUG]` - Debug messages

### Theme Customization

\`\`\`rust
use fastapi_output::prelude::*;

// Use a preset theme
let theme = FastApiTheme::neon();

// Build output with custom theme
let output = RichOutput::builder()
    .mode(OutputMode::Rich)
    .theme(theme)
    .build();

// Access theme colors
println!("Success color: {}", output.theme().success.to_hex());
\`\`\`

+{"id":"bd-pto1","title":"Write documentation and examples","description":"## Task: Documentation and Examples (REVISED)

### Overview
Create comprehensive documentation and examples for the rich output system using the ACTUAL implemented API.

### API Reference (rustdoc)

The main entry points are already documented in lib.rs:
- `RichOutput` - Main facade for output operations
- `OutputMode` - Three modes: Rich, Plain, Minimal
- `Banner` - Server startup banner
- `ErrorFormatter` - Validation and HTTP error formatting
- `RequestLogger` - Request/response logging

### Getting Started Guide

```markdown
# Getting Started with fastapi-output

## Installation

Add to your Cargo.toml:
\`\`\`toml
[dependencies]
fastapi-output = { path = "../crates/fastapi-output" }
\`\`\`

## Basic Usage

### Auto-Detecting Mode
\`\`\`rust
use fastapi_output::prelude::*;

fn main() {
    // Automatically detects agent/CI environment
    let output = RichOutput::auto();

    // Check what mode was selected
    println!("Mode: {}", output.mode_name());
    println!("Agent-friendly: {}", output.is_agent_mode());

    // Print styled messages
    output.success("Server started successfully");
    output.info("Listening on port 8000");
    output.warning("Using default configuration");
    output.error("Failed to connect to database");
}
\`\`\`

### Startup Banner
\`\`\`rust
use fastapi_output::prelude::*;

let output = RichOutput::auto();
let banner = Banner::new(output.mode());

let info = ServerInfo::new(env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION"), "localhost", 8000)
    .docs_path("/docs")
    .redoc_path("/redoc")
    .openapi_path("/openapi.json");

println!("{}", banner.render(&info));
\`\`\`

Output (Plain Mode - for agents):
\`\`\`
================================================================================
                              FastAPI Rust v0.1.0
================================================================================
Server URL: http://localhost:8000
Documentation:
  - Swagger UI:  /docs
  - ReDoc:       /redoc
  - OpenAPI:     /openapi.json
================================================================================
\`\`\`

### Request Logging
\`\`\`rust
use fastapi_output::prelude::*;
use std::time::Duration;

let logger = RequestLogger::new(OutputMode::Plain);

let entry = LogEntry::new(HttpMethod::Get, "/api/users", 200)
    .timing(ResponseTiming::new(Duration::from_millis(45)))
    .client_ip("127.0.0.1");

println!("{}", logger.format(&entry));
// Output: GET /api/users 200 45ms 127.0.0.1
\`\`\`

### Error Formatting
\`\`\`rust
use fastapi_output::prelude::*;

let formatter = ErrorFormatter::new(OutputMode::Plain);

// Validation errors
let errors = vec![
    ValidationErrorDetail::new(
        vec![LocItem::field("body"), LocItem::field("email")],
        "invalid email format",
        "value_error.email",
    ),
];
let result = formatter.format_validation_errors(&errors);
println!("{}", result.plain);

// HTTP errors
let http_err = HttpErrorInfo::new(404, "User not found")
    .path("/api/users/999")
    .method("GET");
println!("{}", formatter.format_http_error(&http_err).plain);
\`\`\`
\`\`\`

### Agent Compatibility Guide

#### How Detection Works

The library checks environment variables in this order:

1. `FASTAPI_OUTPUT_MODE` - Explicit mode (rich/plain/minimal)
2. `FASTAPI_AGENT_MODE=1` - Force agent mode
3. `FASTAPI_HUMAN_MODE=1` - Force human mode
4. `FORCE_COLOR` - Force rich output
5. `NO_COLOR` - Force plain output
6. Agent detection (CLAUDE_CODE, CODEX_CLI, CURSOR_SESSION, etc.)
7. CI detection (CI, GITHUB_ACTIONS, GITLAB_CI, etc.)
8. TTY detection

#### Forcing a Mode
\`\`\`rust
// Always use plain mode
let output = RichOutput::plain();

// Always use rich mode
let output = RichOutput::rich();

// Auto-detect based on environment
let output = RichOutput::auto();

// Change mode at runtime
let mut output = RichOutput::auto();
output.set_mode(OutputMode::Plain);
\`\`\`

#### Plain Mode Output Format

Plain mode uses consistent prefixes for easy parsing:
- `[OK]` - Success messages
- `[ERROR]` - Error messages
- `[WARN]` - Warning messages
- `[INFO]` - Informational messages
- `[DEBUG]` - Debug messages

### Theme Customization

\`\`\`rust
use fastapi_output::prelude::*;

// Use a preset theme
let theme = FastApiTheme::neon();

// Build output with custom theme
let output = RichOutput::builder()
    .mode(OutputMode::Rich)
    .theme(theme)
    .build();

// Access theme colors
println!("Success color: {}", output.theme().success.to_hex());
\`\`\`

### Example Files

```
examples/
├── basic_usage.rs        # RichOutput::auto() and messages
├── startup_banner.rs     # Banner with ServerInfo
├── request_logging.rs    # RequestLogger with LogEntry
├── error_formatting.rs   # ErrorFormatter usage
├── agent_detection.rs    # Mode detection demonstration
├── custom_theme.rs       # Theme customization
└── ci_output.rs          # CI-friendly patterns
```

- [ ] Getting started guide with actual API
- [ ] Request logging examples
- [ ] Error formatting examples
- [ ] Agent detection guide
- [ ] Theme customization examples
- [ ] 7+ example files that compile and run

 {"id":"fastapi_rust-oep","title":"Circular dependency detection","description":"Detect circular dependencies at startup:

DETECTION:
- Build dependency graph during route registration
- Detect cycles using DFS with visited set
- Report clear error: A -> B -> C -> A

ERROR MESSAGE:
'Circular dependency detected: DbPool -> UserService -> DbPool'

WHEN TO CHECK:
- During app.build() / app startup
- Fail fast before accepting requests

IMPLEMENTATION:
- Adjacency list for dependency graph
- Color-based DFS (white/gray/black)
- Report first cycle found

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Circular deps detected at startup
- Clear error message with cycle path
- App fails to start with cycle
- No runtime penalty (startup only)

TEST REQUIREMENTS:
- Unit: Simple A -> B -> A cycle
- Unit: Long cycle A -> B -> C -> D -> A
- Unit: Diamond (no cycle) passes

 {"id":"fastapi_rust-ox6","title":"Custom exception handlers registry","description":"Implement custom exception handler registration:

APP BUILDER API:
app.exception_handler(MyError, my_handler)
app.exception_handler(HttpError, custom_http_handler)

HANDLER SIGNATURE:
async fn handler(ctx: &RequestContext, err: MyError) -> Response

DEFAULT HANDLERS:
- HttpError -> JSON response with status/detail
- RequestValidationError -> 422 with error list
- CancelledError -> 499 Client Closed Request
- Panic (via catch_unwind) -> 500 Internal Server Error

HANDLER CHAIN:
1. Check for exact type match
2. Check for trait object match
3. Fall back to default handler

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Custom handlers can be registered
- Type-safe handler registration
- Default handlers work out of box
- Handler can access RequestContext

TEST REQUIREMENTS:
- Unit: Handler registration
- Unit: Handler invocation
- Integration: Custom error type handling
- Integration: Override default handler","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T15:09:51.267639585Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-21T19:44:34.427649835Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T19:44:34.427602826Z","close_reason":"done","closed_by_session":"SilverHeron-exception-handlers","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["app","errors","phase-2"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-ox6","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-zom","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:33Z","created_by":"import"}]}

async fn login(res: Response) -> Json<Token> {

    Json(token)

Cookie::new(name, value)
    .path('/')
    .domain('.example.com')
    .secure(true)
    .http_only(true)
    .same_site(SameSite::Strict)
    .max_age(Duration::hours(24))

+///
+/// Controls request rate limits using token bucket or sliding window algorithms.

+///
+/// # Defaults

+/// | `algorithm` | `TokenBucket` |
+/// | `include_headers` | `true` |

+///
+/// # Response Headers (when `include_headers` is `true`)
+///
+/// - `X-RateLimit-Limit`: Maximum requests per window
+/// - `X-RateLimit-Remaining`: Remaining requests in current window
+/// - `X-RateLimit-Reset`: Seconds until window resets

+///
+/// # Example
+///
+/// ```ignore
+/// use fastapi_core::middleware::{RateLimitBuilder, RateLimitAlgorithm};
+///

+///     .max_requests(1000)
+///     .window_secs(3600) // 1000 req/hour
+///     .algorithm(RateLimitAlgorithm::SlidingWindow)
+///     .build();
+/// ```
